DECLARATION OF PLAINTIFF IN OPPOS TO DEMURRER FILED BY PLAINTIFF MICHELS PHD, WILLIAM J

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE UNTIL 6/01/15 TO AMEND. AS CURRENTLY PLED, PLAINTIFF'S "FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION" IS BARRED BY THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY PROVISIONS OF THE WORKERS' COMPENSATION ACT AND, AS TO DR. BICKLER, FAILS TO ALLEGE THAT DR. BICKLER WAS A PARTY TO THE CONTRACT OR IS OTHERWISE LIABLE FOR THE ALLEGED BREACH OF THE CONTRACT. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE (UNTIL 6/01/15) TO AMEND, IF HE IS ABLE TO DO, TO ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS SHOWING THAT HIS CLAIM IS NOT BARRED BY THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY PROVISIONS OF THE WORKERS' COMPENSATION ACT AND DR. BICKLER WAS A PARTY TO THE CONTRACT OR IS OTHERWISE LIABLE FOR THE ALLEGED BREACH OF THE CONTRACT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963; DEPT. 302 (302/HK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-13-2015 Continued To Sep-30-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-11-2015 Continued To Jul-29-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff WILLIAM J MICHALES, PHD, Tel No. (415) 524-2816 present in pro per;
Atty. DONALD P. MARGOLIS of the Office of the General Counsel, Tel No. (510) 987-9151 appearing for Defendants Regents of the University of California and Dr. Philip Bickler MD.

RULING - The Court made disclosure to parties regarding friendship with General Counsel of the University of California, and that this friendship would not prevent the Court from being fair or impartial in this matter. The matter was argued and submitted by parties. The Court adopted its tentative ruling (with the sole modification of allowing until 6/1/15 to amend): DEFENDANTS REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AND PHILIP BICKLER MD, PHD'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained. Plaintiff is given leave until 6/1/15 to amend. As currently pled, plaintiff's "fourth cause of action" is barred by the exclusive remedy provisions of the Workers' Compensation Act and, as to Dr. Bickler, fails to allege that Dr. Bickler was a party to the contract or is otherwise liable for the alleged breach of the contract. Plaintiff is granted leave (until 6/1/15) to amend, if he is able to do, to allege ultimate facts showing that his claim is not barred by the exclusive remedy provisions of the Workers' Compensation Act and Dr. Bickler was a party to the contract or is otherwise liable for the alleged breach of the contract. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
